prompts: Common organic compound functional groups include hydroxyl (-OH) found in alcohols, carbonyl (>C=O) in aldehydes and ketones, carboxyl (-COOH) in carboxylic acids, amino (-NH2) in amines, sulfhydryl (-SH) in thiols, phosphate (-PO4) in organic phosphates, ester (-COOR) in esters, ether (-O-) in ethers, and halide (-X) where X is a halogen. These functional groups determine the chemical reactivity and properties of organic compounds, playing a critical role in the study and application of organic chemistry in biological and industrial contexts.
